key_insight: DT-GOL handles concept drift with delayed labels by using real-time feature geometry as a surrogate signal and a dual-track learner to adapt before ground truth arrives.
engineering_relevance: For nonstationary engineering data streams, it offers a practical way to maintain online predictive performance when labels are slow, costly, or intermittently unavailable.
method: Models label delay as a semi-supervised online learning problem.
Uses dynamic geometric evidence to generate soft labels that reduce confirmation bias.
Separates learning into a stable master branch updated by delayed ground truth and a transient branch updated by soft geometric knowledge.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports extensive experiments on real and synthetic datasets, with DT-GOL outperforming state-of-the-art baselines, especially under concept drift.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of geometric surrogates; if feature topology does not track concept change well, early adaptation may become unreliable.
paper_title: DT-GOL: Dual-Track Geometric Online Learning in Nonstationary Environment with Label Delay
